summary:
Despite normal serum potassium levels and absent signs of endocrine disorders, this patient exhibits severe uncontrolled hypertension likely driven by underlying vascular pathology such as benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis from chronic hypoperfusion. This condition reflects long-standing inadequate renal perfusion leading to increased peripheral resistance and persistent elevation in systemic arterial pressure despite current medications.
